titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Compton Mackenzie Papers
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Tuesday March 10, 2020 at 03:00

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Maggs Brothers Ltd. on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2012.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into four series: I. Writings, 1913 - 1982. II. Correspondence, 1892 - 1970. III. Personal papers, 1932. IV. Clippings and printed material, 1950 - 1965

bioghist.head: Compton Mackenzie
bioghist.p: Sir Edward Montague Compton Mackenzie (1883-1972) was a Scottish writer, critic, and Scottish nationalist. In 1928 he co-founded the Scottish National Party with Hugh MacDiarmid, RB Cunninghame Graham, and John MacCormick.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Compton Mackenzie Papers. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of writings, correspondence, scripts, personal papers, and clippings by or related to the life and work of Compton Mackenzie.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Compton Mackenzie Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials roughly maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains typescripts, writings, articles, and related papers.

did.unittitle: Article for Associated Press--typescript and holograph manuscripts with related correspondence
BBC script -- "Pouishnoff Plays to Compton Mackenzie"
BBC script -- "In Tune With The Times"
BBC scripts -- "The Seventh Man," "The Ingoldsby Legend," and "Sir Max Beerbohm's 80th Birthday"
BBC scripts and articles regarding Robert Louis Stevenson with related correspondence .
BBC scripts -- "Salute to Compton Mackenzie by Moray McLaren" and "This Week's Good Cause" with related correspondence
BBC scripts -- "The Celestial Onion" and "A Tribute to the Memory of the Late Majesty Queen Mary"
BBC scripts -- "The Island Life" and "European Service: An Illustrated Talk by Kenneth Young" with related correspondence
Typescripts of "Butterfly Hill" and "The Secret Island"
"Told by the Coddy: Tales of Barra" typescript manuscript of introduction by J.L. Campbell
Henry Collins correspondence with "Foreward" typescript and holograph manuscripts
"Five Minutes With Sir Compton Mackenzie" typescript manuscript by John Connell
"Dining Room Battle" typescript and holograph manuscripts
"Norman Douglas Memoriam Talk" typescript and holograph manuscripts
Typescript article on early writing life
"The House of Maxwell" forward typescript and holograph manuscripts
"Greek Stories for Aldus," including typescript and holograph manuscripts of "Jason" and "Perseus"
"Greek Stories for Aldus," including typescript and holograph manuscripts of "Jason" and "Perseus"
"The Legend of Theseus" and "The Legend of Perseus" typescripts with related correspondence
Press cutting on Lord of the Isles
Articles on gardening and "The Lost Cause" play
Article on Compton Mackenzie and "The Magic of the Hebrides" review by Compton Mackenzie
"Compton Mackenzie" typescript by G.H. Doane; "Cats" typescript; and "Butterfly Hill" typescript
W. Somerset Maugham correspondence and related papers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes "Paper" typescript and holograph manuscripts, along with "Appendix: Murder" typescript.

did.unittitle: "Monarch of the Glen" film version holograph manuscript
Articles on John McCormick, Irish tenor
"My Life and Times: Vol. 1" bound typescript
"A Visit to Ireland" unpublished article on Octave 6
"A Polish Holiday" holograph manuscript and "Gramophone Editorial" holograph manuscript
"Execution of Mary Queen of Scotts" typescript and correspondence related to Scottish history
"The Purple Emperor" typescript and holograph manuscripts
BBC radio scripts with related correspondence

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains scripts of "The Value of Satire;" "John Richardson of Nicobars;" "The Ragpicker's Cat." Contains forward manuscripts of "This Britain," and "Carnival."

did.unittitle: "Welcome to Bonnie Scotland" forward typescript and typescript regarding John McCormick
"The Old Men of the Sea" and "The Goosegirls" short story typescripts
Untitled article on Robert Louis Stevenson
"In My Library" holograph manuscript and "R.L.S.--Heir of the Romantics" article
"Robert Louis Stevenson" page proof with related correspondence
"Life of Robert Louis Stevenson" holograph manuscript
"The Secret Island" typescript and holograph manuscripts
"The Strongest Man on Earth" holograph manuscript
"The Strongest Man on Earth" galleys and typescript
Holograph manuscript concerning tax and copyright
Holograph article on TV and BBC script of "Song Without End" review
Untitled unpublished article regarding "Whisky Galore" and related correspondence with Sunday Express
"Water on the Brain" play typescript with related correspondence
"The Very Devil" holograph manuscript, holograph manuscript of article on Norman D., and related correspondence
"Compton Mackenzie On Books" typescript and holograph manuscripts for Harper's Bazaar
Typescripts and page proofs of other writers' works with related correspondence

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains page proofs of "The Bell" by M. Gowanie Williams; "Compton Mackenzie" by Kenneth Young; "Standing Down" by Richard Hunt; "Calvary" by Compton Mackenzie and Faith Compton Mackenzie; and "A Bird on the Wing is Passing" musical score.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials roughly maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence between Mackenzie and others, along with related papers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence with R.H. Bridgman-Evans, M.C.; Dr. White and Audrey Battersby; Charles Grover; R.J. Minney; Mosdair Milne; Thelma Cazalat-Keir; Fanny Mae Taggart; J. Thomas McLaughlin; Michael Foor; Elena Ivanova; Elliot Morgan; Adrian Conan Doyle; Dom Pierre-Sylvestre Houedard; Laurence Olivier; Sir Fitzroy Maclean; Draper Hill Jr.; and Francis Noel-Baker.

did.unittitle: "Broken Promise: Angus Wilson on the English Novel" article with related correspondence
Correspondence with John Macpherson and others with related papers

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials roughly maintain order found at time of acquisition.

did.unittitle: "REX v. Compton Mackenzie" trial transcripts and depositions

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials roughly maintain order found at time of acquisition.
